title: Operation-Invoker Binding-Selection Conformance Fixture
description: JSON Schema describing the format of a binding-selection conformance fixture file (one rule-cluster of the operation-invoker contract's selection prose per file).

cluster.description: Slug naming the rule-cluster of the selection contract this file covers (e.g., 'default-tier', 'override-selection'). Matches the file's basename.

description.description: Human-readable statement of the rule-cluster, ideally byte-faithful to the operation-invoker contract's prose.

notes.description: Optional file-level rationale about the cluster's coverage in this corpus. Informational; not consumed by harnesses.

tests.description: Test cases for this rule-cluster. Each case bundles an OBI document, an invocation addressing (operation, or operation plus explicit binding), the notional invoker's supported binding-specification set, and the expected selection outcome.

document.description: A complete, valid OpenBindings interface document, embedded inline. Harnesses MUST run it through their implementation's real document validation before selecting; a fixture whose document does not validate is a corpus defect.

operation.description: The operation identifier the invocation addresses (a key or alias, resolved per OBI-T-12). When 'binding' is also present, this is the operation the binding derives to: the wire contract's OperationInvocationInput carries operation and binding mutually exclusively (the operation is derived from an explicit binding), and the fixture carries the derived operation so operation-keyed native APIs can drive the same scenario. Fixture invariant: document.bindings[binding].operation resolves to this operation (vacuous when the binding key is undefined).

supported.description: The notional invoker's supported set: exact binding-specification identifiers it can act on (natively or via a delegate). The candidate set is the operation's bindings whose source's governing binding specification appears here, by exact identifier.

selection.description: The context.configuration.selection consumer override: an ordered list of binding keys, the first invocable entry winning, the default policy applying when none is. Absent means no override is configured; an empty list configures an override with no invocable entry, which also falls through to the default policy.

binding.description: Explicit binding key: the wire contract's binding-addressed form. Selection (default policy and any configured override) is bypassed entirely; the named binding is used even if deprecated, and an unknown key is an error.

expected.description: The selection outcome: either the key of the selected binding, or an error.

binding.description: The binding key selection resolves to.

error.description: Selection fails. The contract maps both kinds to a terminal error with code ERR_BINDING_NOT_FOUND.

kind.description: Which rule failed: 'unknown-binding' (an explicit binding key that is not defined on the interface), or 'no-candidate' (no invocable binding exists for the operation). Informative for harness reporting; both surface as ERR_BINDING_NOT_FOUND.
